chunks: Photosynthesis is how green plants make their own food.
They use energy from sunlight to do it.
The green pigment chlorophyll captures that light energy.
Chlorophyll sits inside tiny parts of the cell called chloroplasts.
Plants take in carbon dioxide from the air.
They take in water from the soil.
Using light, they turn these into glucose and oxygen.
Glucose is the plant's food. Oxygen is released into the air.
